Current: const API_PATH: &str = "/services/data/v59.0";

Salesforce releases 3 API versions per year (Spring, Summer, Winter). v59.0 corresponds to Winter '24. As of 2026, the current version is likely v62.0 or higher. Older versions are eventually retired. Check https://developer.salesforce.com/docs/atlas.en-us.api_rest.meta/api_rest/dome_versions.htm

Steps:
1. Check current Salesforce REST API version
2. Update API_PATH constant
3. Make version configurable (Salesforce orgs may need to pin specific versions)
4. Add version discovery: Salesforce provides GET /services/data/ which returns available versions
5.  {"id":"flywheel_connectors-292","title":"fcp.jira: JQL query execution engine","description":"# fcp.jira: JQL Query Execution Engine

## Goal
Provide a safe, typed, and ergonomic JQL query layer for search and automation.

## External surface (NetworkConstraints)
Default deny.
- Network egress MUST be restricted per-operation via manifest `NetworkConstraints`.
- Allow only the configured service host(s); HTTPS-only where applicable; deny redirects by default.
- Bound timeouts + response sizes; treat all remote responses as untrusted input; never leak secrets/PII in logs or errors.

## Zone model (mechanical)

<!-- fcp2-single-zone-note -->
**FCP2 single-zone binding note:** A connector instance binds to exactly one `ZoneId` for its entire lifetime.
The connector MUST NOT multiplex multiple zones internally.

Recommended deployment pattern: run separate connector instances in separate zones with different capability grants/policies.

## Scope
Operations (representative):
- `jira.jql.search`
- `jira.jql.validate`
- `jira.jql.autocomplete`

## Key requirements
- Capability gated (`jira.issues.read`).
- Query safety:
  - strict escaping/encoding of user-provided values
  - prevent injection into raw JQL strings where possible (prefer builder)
- Pagination:
  - deterministic iteration over `startAt`/`maxResults`
  - stable ordering requirements when paginating
- Performance:
  - request field subsets; avoid fetching full issue bodies unless required

## Tests
- Mock-only tests cover:
  - query builder correctness
  - pagination edge cases
  - invalid JQL errors
  - rate limits

## Acceptance Criteria
- Agents can run complex searches reliably with predictable pagination.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-298cj","title":"[PERF] fcp-streaming::From<Message> for WsMessage: recv path copies every binary frame","description":"crates/fcp-streaming/src/websocket.rs:362-389. Converting fastwebsockets::Message into WsMessage clones every Binary/Ping/Pong payload with to_vec(), and recv() uses that conversion on the inbound hot path before handing frames to callers. Complexity is O(frame_len) extra memcpy and allocation for every non-text frame, even though the upstream representation is already bytes-backed. Proposed improvement: store binary/control payloads as Bytes (or a borrowed/owned enum) inside WsMessage so recv/send can preserve zero-copy buffers instead of round-tripping through Vec<u8>.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:35.406028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:35.406028Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Converting fastwebsockets::Message into WsMessage clones every Binary/Ping/Pong payload with to_vec(), and recv() uses that conversion on the inbound hot path before handing frames to callers. Complexity is O(frame_len) extra memcpy and allocation for every non-text frame, even though the upstream representation is already bytes-backed. Proposed improvement: store binary/control payloads as Bytes (or a borrowed/owned enum) inside WsMessage so recv/send can preserve zero-copy buffers instead of round-tripping through Vec<u8>.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:35.406028Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:13:11.300193Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

Encourage connectors to reference `limits.rs` in validation logic.

- Documentation on how to update limits.

- At least one connector example uses the limits module.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]

- Use sequential test budgets with e-value thresholds to stop early when safe.

- Cache canonical forms to avoid repeated parse cost.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-2ady","title":"[POL] Tests: unit + integration","description":"# [POL] Tests: unit + integration

## Goal
Comprehensive test suite for policy bundle workflows.

## Unit Tests
- Bundle signature verify/fail cases.
- Diff stability and risk summary correctness.
- Preview determinism and reason code stability.

## Integration Tests
- Apply a bundle and verify enforcement changes.
- Rollback restores prior behavior.

## Logging Requirements
- Structured JSON logs for each test stage (bundle_id, result, duration_ms).

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deterministic outputs with stable ordering.
- Clear, redacted logs suitable for audit.","notes":"[AlienArtifactPractical]
- Add evidence ledger fields with per-check log-odds contributions and explicit loss decision.
- Add anytime-valid risk control (e-process) for approvals/denials and record epsilon in receipts.
[ExtremeOptimizationPractical]
- Compile policies to DAG/BDD and cache allow/deny results with LRU.
- Use bitset capability sets for fast subset checks.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-aknsx","title":"fcp-mesh: rejected symbol requests still debit peer budgets before proof gate","description":"crates/fcp-mesh/src/symbol_request.rs:300-328

Excerpt:
```
admission.check_admission(...)?;
admission.record_bytes(peer, estimated_response_bytes, now_ms);
admission.record_symbols(peer, max_response_symbols, now_ms);
... if max_response_symbols > self.policy.require_proof_of_need_above && !has_proof_of_need && !is_authenticated { return Err(ProofOfNeedRequired) }
```

Finding: the handler mutates per-peer byte/symbol budgets before the later proof-of-need rejection branch runs. A peer can send large unauthenticated requests that are ultimately rejected and still consume budget state, turning a policy reject into a state mutation side effect.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T10:45:27.427800Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:01.497387Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T10:46:01.497069Z","close_reason":"Moved symbol-request budget debits behind proof-of-need rejection so rejected requests stay side-effect free.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-aoqb2","title":"gmail manifest/compliance harness misses real connector surface","description":"Evidence:

- crates/fcp-e2e/tests/gmail_compliance_e2e.rs:174-180 uses tests/vectors/manifest/manifest_valid.toml instead of connectors/gmail/manifest.toml, so the compliance suite cannot catch the drift.

- operations_info / manifest contract is stale for gmail.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-asf","title":"fcp.spotify: Recommendations Engine","description":"# fcp.spotify: Recommendations Engine (FCP2)

## Goal
Expose Spotify recommendation surfaces to support:
- playlist generation
- discovery workflows

## Scope
- Get recommendations from seeds (tracks/artists/genres).
- List available genre seeds.

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `spotify.recommendations.read`

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned.
- Avoid logging raw seed lists if they could reveal user taste (privacy).

## Deliverables
- Operation schemas:
  - `spotify.recommendations.get`
  - `spotify.recommendations.genres.list`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - query parameter formation
  - error mapping

## Acceptance criteria
- Recommendation queries are reliable and correctly typed.

-{"id":"flywheel_connectors-atd32","title":"[PERF] fcp-audit::verify_chain_with_signers: canonicalizes every entry twice","description":"crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:278-325 and 1713-1744. verify_chain_with_signers() first calls entry.verify_signature() for every entry, and verify_signature() rebuilds signing_bytes(), which recomputes computed_id() and canonical CBOR for the whole entry payload. After that loop, verify_chain_with_signers() calls verify_chain(), which recomputes computed_id() for every entry again. Complexity is therefore roughly 2 full canonical-serialize+hash passes per entry before any head checks, which is avoidable on large chains or fat metadata payloads. Proposed improvement: compute canonical ids once up front and thread them through both signature verification and structural verification, or extend verify_chain() to accept precomputed canonical ids so signer-aware verification does not pay the full serialization cost twice.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:18.543765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:18.543765Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
+{"id":"flywheel_connectors-atd32","title":"[PERF] fcp-audit::verify_chain_with_signers: canonicalizes every entry twice","description":"crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:278-325 and 1713-1744. verify_chain_with_signers() first calls entry.verify_signature() for every entry, and verify_signature() rebuilds signing_bytes(), which recomputes computed_id() and canonical CBOR for the whole entry payload. After that loop, verify_chain_with_signers() calls verify_chain(), which recomputes computed_id() for every entry again. Complexity is therefore roughly 2 full canonical-serialize+hash passes per entry before any head checks, which is avoidable on large chains or fat metadata payloads. Proposed improvement: compute canonical ids once up front and thread them through both signature verification and structural verification, or extend verify_chain() to accept precomputed canonical ids so signer-aware verification does not pay the full serialization cost twice.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:56:18.543765Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T21:17:14.067393Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T21:17:14.066923Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2837,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-atd32","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in commit f30b22c7.

Fix: canonicalize each entry once, share across signature + chain-integrity passes.

New pub API (additive only):
- AuditEntry::signing_bytes_from_id(id) -> Vec<u8> (infallible; builds transcript from precomputed id)
- AuditEntry::verify_signature_with_id(key, id) -> Result<(), AuditError>
- verify_chain_with_precomputed_ids(entries, head, zone, ids: &[Result<String, AuditError>]) -> VerifyReport

verify_chain_with_signers now:
1. Builds precomputed_ids once via entries.iter().map(AuditEntry::computed_id).collect().
2. Iterates entries.zip(precomputed_ids) calling verify_signature_with_id(&key, &id) — no per-entry canonicalize.
3. Delegates to verify_chain_with_precomputed_ids with the same slice.

Net: 2 full canonical-CBOR+BLAKE3 passes per entry → 1. Public verify_chain and every other call site unchanged (byte-identical).

AuditError already derives Clone, so threading Result<String, AuditError> through both loops needs no type changes.

Regression tests:
- signing_bytes_from_id_matches_signing_bytes_for_signed_entry: pins byte-identical transcript + both verify paths accept legitimate signature and reject wrong key.
- verify_chain_with_precomputed_ids_matches_verify_chain: pins VerifyReport equivalence over a 4-entry signed chain.

Verified: cargo test -p fcp-audit — 382 pass (380 baseline + 2 new regressions), zero failures.

— cod_4 / SunnyMoose","created_at":"2026-04-23T21:17:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-atmv","title":"[FCP2] Bootstrap/Genesis: First-Run Workflow, Owner Key Ceremony, Initial Enrollment","description":"
## Cold Recovery (Zero-Peer Disaster Recovery)

When ALL peers are gone and only the recovery phrase exists:

```rust
pub enum RecoveryMode {
    /// Found existing genesis at a peer
    Rejoin { peer_addr: SocketAddr, genesis_fingerprint: String },
    /// No peers found, cold start from recovery phrase only
    ColdStart { recovery_phrase: RecoveryPhrase },
}

pub struct ColdRecovery {
    /// Derived owner keypair from phrase
    pub owner_keypair: SigningKeypair,
    /// Recreated genesis (MUST match original fingerprint)
    pub genesis: GenesisState,
    /// Warning flags
    pub warnings: Vec<ColdRecoveryWarning>,
}

pub enum ColdRecoveryWarning {
    /// No audit history available
    NoAuditHistory,
    /// Cannot verify revocations (no peers)
    RevocationStateUnknown,
    /// Starting with single node (no quorum)
    SingleNodeStart,
}

impl ColdRecovery {
    /// Recover from phrase when no peers exist
    /// CRITICAL: This creates a fresh genesis with the same owner key.
    /// Any objects/zones created after original genesis are LOST.
    pub fn from_phrase(
        phrase: &RecoveryPhrase,
        expected_fingerprint: Option<&str>,
    ) -> Result<Self, ColdRecoveryError> {
        // 1. Derive owner keypair
        let owner_keypair = phrase.derive_owner_keypair();

        // 2. Recreate genesis (deterministic from owner pubkey)
        let genesis = GenesisState::create(owner_keypair.public())?;

        // 3. Verify fingerprint if provided
        if let Some(expected) = expected_fingerprint {
            if genesis.fingerprint() != expected {
                return Err(ColdRecoveryError::FingerprintMismatch {
                    expected: expected.to_string(),
                    actual: genesis.fingerprint(),
                });
            }
        }

        // 4. Warn about limitations
        let warnings = vec![
            ColdRecoveryWarning::NoAuditHistory,
            ColdRecoveryWarning::RevocationStateUnknown,
            ColdRecoveryWarning::SingleNodeStart,
        ];

        Ok(Self { owner_keypair, genesis, warnings })
    }
}
```

### Cold Recovery CLI Flow
```bash
# When no peers are reachable
fcp recover --recovery-phrase --cold-start
# Enter your 24-word recovery phrase:
# > abandon ability...
#
# ⚠️  COLD START RECOVERY
# No existing mesh peers found. This will:
# - Create fresh genesis from owner key
# - Lose any objects created after original bootstrap
# - Lose audit history
# - Start with single node (no quorum)
#
# Do you have a backup of your .fcp directory? [y/N]:
# If you have a recent backup, restore it instead.
#
# Expected fingerprint (leave empty to skip): SHA256:xYzAbC...
# ✓ Fingerprint verified
# ✓ Cold start recovery complete
```

## Bootstrap Idempotency (NORMATIVE)

```rust
pub enum InitResult {
    /// Fresh initialization completed
    Created(GenesisState),
    /// Genesis already exists at this location
    AlreadyExists {
        fingerprint: String,
        genesis_time: DateTime<Utc>,
        suggestion: InitSuggestion,
    },
    /// Partial state detected (crashed during previous init)
    PartialState {
        phase: BootstrapPhase,
        suggestion: PartialStateSuggestion,
    },
}

pub enum InitSuggestion {
    /// Use existing genesis (no action needed)
    UseExisting,
    /// Run with --force to overwrite (DANGEROUS)
    ForceOverwrite,
    /// Run in a different directory
    UseDifferentPath,
}

pub enum PartialStateSuggestion {
    /// Resume from where we left off
    Resume,
    /// Clean up and start fresh
    CleanAndRetry,
}

impl BootstrapPhase {
    /// Check for partial state from crashed init
    pub fn detect_partial() -> Option<Self> {
        // Check for:
        // - .fcp/init.lock file
        // - .fcp/genesis.partial directory
        // - Uncommitted key material
        todo!()
    }
}
```

**Idempotency Rules:**
1. `fcp init` on existing mesh → Error with `AlreadyExists`
2. `fcp init --force` → Overwrites (requires `--i-understand-this-is-destructive`)
3. `fcp init` after crash → Detects partial state, offers resume or clean

## Ceremony Abort/Resume Protocol

```rust
pub struct CeremonyCheckpoint {
    /// Ceremony ID
    pub ceremony_id: CeremonyId,
    /// Current phase
    pub phase: CeremonyPhase,
    /// Collected commitments so far
    pub commitments: HashMap<ParticipantId, FrostCommitment>,
    /// Collected shares so far
    pub shares: HashMap<ParticipantId, EncryptedShare>,
    /// Checkpoint timestamp
    pub checkpoint_at: DateTime<Utc>,
    /// Timeout for this phase
    pub phase_deadline: DateTime<Utc>,
}

impl ThresholdCeremony {
    /// Abort ceremony with reason
    pub fn abort(&mut self, reason: &str) -> CeremonyAbortResult {
        let phase_before_abort = self.phase.clone();
        self.phase = CeremonyPhase::Failed {
            reason: reason.to_string(),
            at_phase: format!("{:?}", phase_before_abort),
        };

        // Notify all participants
        self.broadcast_abort(reason);

        // Save abort to transcript
        self.transcript.phases.push(PhaseRecord {
            phase: "Failed".to_string(),
            entered_at: Utc::now(),
            reason: Some(reason.to_string()),
        });

        CeremonyAbortResult {
            ceremony_id: self.ceremony_id.clone(),
            can_resume: self.can_resume_after_abort(&phase_before_abort),
            checkpoint: self.create_checkpoint(),
        }
    }

    /// Resume ceremony from checkpoint
    pub fn resume(checkpoint: CeremonyCheckpoint) -> Result<Self, CeremonyResumeError> {
        // Validate checkpoint is not expired
        if checkpoint.phase_deadline < Utc::now() {
            return Err(CeremonyResumeError::CheckpointExpired);
        }

        // Reconstruct ceremony state
        let mut ceremony = Self::new(
            checkpoint.ceremony_id.threshold,
            checkpoint.ceremony_id.total,
        );
        ceremony.ceremony_id = checkpoint.ceremony_id;
        ceremony.phase = checkpoint.phase;
        // ... restore other state

        Ok(ceremony)
    }

    fn can_resume_after_abort(&self, phase: &CeremonyPhase) -> bool {
        // Can resume from Gathering or Round1
        // Cannot resume from Round2 (shares may be exposed)
        matches!(phase,
            CeremonyPhase::Gathering { .. } |
            CeremonyPhase::Round1Commitments { .. }
        )
    }
}
```

### Ceremony CLI Abort/Resume
```bash
# Abort a stuck ceremony
fcp ceremony abort <ceremony_id> --reason "Participant Bob unreachable"

# Resume ceremony from checkpoint
fcp ceremony resume <ceremony_id>
# ✓ Loaded checkpoint from phase: Round1Commitments
# ✓ 2/3 commitments already collected
# Waiting for remaining participants...
```

## Time Source Validation

```rust
pub struct TimeValidation {
    /// System time at bootstrap
    pub system_time: DateTime<Utc>,
    /// NTP time (if available)
    pub ntp_time: Option<DateTime<Utc>>,
    /// Drift detected
    pub drift: Option<Duration>,
    /// Validation result
    pub result: TimeValidationResult,
}

pub enum TimeValidationResult {
    /// Time is valid
    Valid,
    /// Slight drift, warn user
    DriftWarning { drift: Duration },
    /// Significant drift, block bootstrap
    DriftError { drift: Duration },
    /// Cannot validate (no network)
    CannotValidate,
}

impl TimeValidation {
    pub fn check() -> Self {
        let system_time = Utc::now();

        // Try NTP check (with short timeout)
        let ntp_time = Self::ntp_check_with_timeout(Duration::from_secs(2));

        let (drift, result) = match ntp_time {
            Some(ntp) => {
                let drift = (system_time - ntp).abs();
                let result = if drift < Duration::from_secs(30) {
                    TimeValidationResult::Valid
                } else if drift < Duration::from_secs(300) {
                    TimeValidationResult::DriftWarning { drift }
                } else {
                    TimeValidationResult::DriftError { drift }
                };
                (Some(drift), result)
            }
            None => (None, TimeValidationResult::CannotValidate),
        };

        Self { system_time, ntp_time, drift, result }
    }
}

// During bootstrap:
pub fn bootstrap_with_time_check(mode: BootstrapMode) -> Result<GenesisState, BootstrapError> {
    let time_check = TimeValidation::check();

    match time_check.result {
        TimeValidationResult::DriftError { drift } => {
            return Err(BootstrapError::TimeSkew {
                drift,
                suggestion: "Synchronize system clock before bootstrap",
            });
        }
        TimeValidationResult::DriftWarning { drift } => {
            tracing::warn!(
                drift_seconds = drift.as_secs(),
                "System clock drift detected, genesis timestamps may be inaccurate"
            );
        }
        _ => {}
    }

    // Continue with bootstrap...
    todo!()
}
```

pub struct DetectedToken {
    pub provider: String,
    pub slot: u32,
    pub label: String,
    pub manufacturer: String,
    pub serial: String,
    pub mechanisms: Vec<String>,
}

pub fn detect_all_tokens() -> Vec<DetectedToken> {
    #[cfg(target_os = "windows")]
    let tokens = windows_pkcs11::detect_tokens();

    #[cfg(target_os = "macos")]
    let tokens = macos_pkcs11::detect_tokens();

    #[cfg(target_os = "linux")]
    let tokens = linux_pkcs11::detect_tokens();

    tokens
}

#[cfg(test)]
mod deterministic_tests {
    use rand_chacha::ChaChaRng;
    use rand::SeedableRng;

    #[test]
    fn test_recovery_phrase_derivation_golden_vectors() {
        // Test known phrase -> keypair derivations
        let phrase = RecoveryPhrase::from_words(&[
            "abandon", "abandon", "abandon", "abandon",
            "abandon", "abandon", "abandon", "abandon",
            "abandon", "abandon", "abandon", "about",
            // ... 24 words
        ]).unwrap();

        let keypair = phrase.derive_owner_keypair();
        assert_eq!(
            hex::encode(keypair.public().as_bytes()),
            "expected_hex_pubkey"
        );
    }

    #[test]
    fn test_frost_share_generation_deterministic() {
        let mut rng = ChaChaRng::seed_from_u64(12345);
        let setup1 = ThresholdOwnerSetup::generate_with_rng(2, 3, &mut rng);

        let mut rng = ChaChaRng::seed_from_u64(12345);
        let setup2 = ThresholdOwnerSetup::generate_with_rng(2, 3, &mut rng);

        // Same seed should produce same shares
        assert_eq!(setup1.group_public_key, setup2.group_public_key);
    }
}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-atva7","title":"[REVIEW][MEDIUM] raptorq envelope decrypt fuzz corpus omits two declared tamper branches","description":"fuzz/fuzz_targets/raptorq_envelope_decrypt.rs:35-37 and 166-179 define CiphertextTruncate and ObjectIdBitflip mutation paths, but fuzz/corpus/fuzz_raptorq_envelope_decrypt only contains identity_chacha.json plus eight tamper seeds (auth_tag, ciphertext, epoch_id, esi, frame_seq, k, sender_instance_id, wrong_zone_key) with no seed for truncate or object_id tampering. Bug scenario: short smoke runs only exercise the checked-in corpus and therefore never hit two negative-authentication branches that the harness claims to cover, so regressions in ciphertext truncation or object-id AAD binding can land unnoticed. Suggested fix: add deterministic JSON seeds for CiphertextTruncate and ObjectIdBitflip and align the commit or bead seed-count language with the actual tamper corpus size.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T19:59:56.849005Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:09.493644Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:31:09.492687Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":2830,"issue_id":"flywheel_connectors-atva7","author":"jemanuel","text":"Resolved in the commit above. Added two deterministic seeds matching the existing corpus style:
- tamper_ciphertext_truncate.json: ChaCha20Poly1305, mutation_index=0, k=5, esi=11, plaintext_len=128 (non-empty plaintext so the truncate arm hits the else branch at fuzz_targets/raptorq_envelope_decrypt.rs:170 rather than the empty-data push).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ax97w","title":"[REVIEW-P5][HIGH] fcp-audit::verify_chain_with_signers accepts unsigned ChainHead quorum","description":"crates/fcp-audit/src/lib.rs:1606-1624 says verify_chain_with_signers verifies both integrity and authenticity, but it only resolves and verifies AuditEntry signatures before delegating to verify_chain() at 1323-1571. verify_chain() checks head linkage and signature_count consistency only; it never cryptographically verifies ChainHead.signatures (see ChainHead fields at 639-658 and head validation at 1498-1521). An attacker who can tamper with the serialized ChainHead can replace the quorum signatures with arbitrary bytes while preserving head_entry/head_seq/zone_id/signature_count, and verify_chain_with_signers still returns Ok(report). Real behavior should verify every counted ChainHead signature against trusted issuers before accepting chain authenticity.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-23T20:41:56.381929Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:09.849524Z","closed_at":"2026-04-23T20:49:09.849225Z","close_reason":"Added ChainHead::signing_bytes() (CHAIN_HEAD_SIG_DOMAIN transcript over zone_id + head_entry + head_seq + coverage_bps + epoch_id + signature_count, EXCLUDES signatures field to break recursion) and ChainHead::verify_signatures() that cryptographically verifies each HeadSignature in the quorum against the caller-supplied issuer key registry. verify_chain_with_signers now calls head.verify_signatures() before delegating to verify_chain, so an attacker who tampers with a serialized ChainHead can no longer pass authentication by swapping quorum signatures for arbitrary bytes. Fail paths: SignerMissing (empty signatures list), UnknownIssuer (kid not in resolver), SignatureInvalid (wrong-length bytes / kid mismatch / Ed25519 verify fails). 5 new regression tests: accepts_signed_head, rejects_head_with_no_signatures (the canonical tamper payload from the bead), rejects_head_with_forged_signature_bytes, rejects_head_signed_by_unknown_issuer, and chain_head_signing_bytes_changes_on_any_covered_field_tamper (zone_id, head_seq, coverage, signature_count, epoch_id, head_entry all detect tamper). Verified: 380 fcp-audit lib tests pass (was 375 + 5 new); all 7 verify_chain_with_signers tests pass.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-ep21.2","title":"fcp.asana: Provisioning Automation","description":"## Goal
Automate provisioning and configuration with minimal human steps.

This bead owns:
- auth setup (OAuth / API keys / webhook secrets) via `CredentialObject`/`CredentialId` (egress proxy injection preferred)
- endpoint configuration
- `fcp doctor` readiness checks
- deterministic, mockable provisioning flows

## FCP2 requirements
- Secrets never touch disk.
- Provisioning is deterministic and testable.
- Error messages are actionable and do not leak secrets/PII.

## Acceptance Criteria
- No real external calls in unit tests.
- Provisioning emits structured logs per `flywheel_connectors-1n78.35`.

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-epqh","title":"FCP Connector Portfolio: Strategic Prioritization Framework","description":"# FCP Connector Portfolio: Strategic Prioritization Framework

## Goal
Define and maintain a rigorous prioritization framework for the connector portfolio (value, risk, effort, dependencies) so roadmap decisions are coherent, explainable, and user-focused.

## Executive Summary

This document captures the strategic thinking, prioritization rationale, and implementation roadmap for the Flywheel Connector Platform (FCP) connector portfolio. The goal is to maximize agent autonomy and capability while building a sustainable, high-quality connector ecosystem.

---

## Strategic Vision

### The Flywheel Effect
Connectors create a flywheel of value:
1. **More connectors** → Agents can do more things
2. **More capable agents** → More users adopt the platform
3. **More users** → More demand for connectors
4. **More demand** → Justification for building more connectors

### Design Principles
- **Quality over quantity**: Each connector should be production-ready, not a proof-of-concept
- **Ecosystem coherence**: Connectors should work together, enabling cross-service workflows
- **Security by default**: Default-deny, capability-based permissions throughout
- **Future-proof**: FCP2 spec compliance ensures consistency and upgradeability

---

## Prioritization Framework

### Value Assessment Model
Each connector is scored on five dimensions:

| Dimension | Weight | Meaning |
|-----------|--------|---------|
| **User Reach** | 25% | How many users need this service |
| **Agent Autonomy** | 25% | How much more can agents do with this |
| **Flywheel Synergy** | 20% | Integration potential with other connectors |
| **Strategic Position** | 15% | Competitive differentiation |
| **Implementation Cost** | 15% | Effort vs. payoff ratio |

### Tier Definitions

#### Tier 1: Critical Infrastructure (Score 93-100)
**Build immediately. Foundation for everything else.**

These connectors either:
- Enable the core agent workflow loop
- Provide capabilities no other connector can substitute
- Have massive user reach (>50% of target users)

Connectors:
- `fcp.twitter` (98) — Real-time social layer, high visibility
- `fcp.linear` (97) — Modern issue tracking, Beads sync
- `fcp.stripe` (96) — Payments/billing, revenue operations
- `fcp.youtube` (95) — Video/transcript access, research
- `fcp.browser` (95) — Universal fallback, any website
- `fcp.plaid` (94) — Banking/financial data
- `fcp.anthropic` (94) — Core AI provider
- `fcp.openai` (94) — Broadest AI capabilities
- `fcp.google-ai` (94) — Massive context, multimodal
- `fcp.vectordb` (93) — Memory/search infrastructure

#### Tier 2: High-Value Productivity (Score 83-92)
**Build in phase 2. Major capability expansion.**

These connectors:
- Serve large user segments
- Enable important workflow categories
- Have strong synergies with Tier 1

Connectors:
- `fcp.microsoft365` (91) — Enterprise productivity suite
- `fcp.jira` (89) — Enterprise issue tracking
- `fcp.twilio` (88) — Communications infrastructure
- `fcp.hubspot` (87) — CRM/marketing automation
- `fcp.airtable` (86) — No-code database
- `fcp.figma` (85) — Design collaboration
- `fcp.sentry` (85) — Error tracking/debugging
- `fcp.datadog` (84) — Observability platform
- `fcp.grafana` (83) — Open-source observability

#### Tier 3: Specialized Value (Score 70-82)
**Build in phase 3. Domain-specific capabilities.**

These connectors:
- Serve specific user segments very well
- Enable specialized workflows
- May be safety-critical (credentials, infrastructure)

Connectors:
- `fcp.kubernetes` (82) — Container orchestration
- `fcp.s3` (81) — Object storage
- `fcp.terraform` (80) — Infrastructure as code
- `fcp.1password` (79) — Credential management
- `fcp.arxiv` (78) — Research papers
- `fcp.zendesk` (77) — Customer support
- `fcp.semanticscholar` (77) — Citation analysis
- `fcp.annas-archive` (76) — Book/document search
- `fcp.reddit` (76) — Community discussions
- `fcp.docusign` (75) — E-signatures
- `fcp.homeassistant` (74) — Home automation
- `fcp.spotify` (72) — Music/podcast

#### Meta-Connectors: Force Multipliers
**Build throughout. Infrastructure for the infrastructure.**

These aren't service connectors but platform capabilities:
- `fcp.webhook-receiver` (88) — Inbound event gateway
- `fcp.cron` (86) — Scheduled task runner
- `fcp.mcp-bridge` — MCP server passthrough

---

## Flywheel Ecosystem Synergies

### Synergy Map

```
                    Agent Mail (coordination hub)
                           ↑
         ┌─────────────────┼─────────────────┐
         ↓                 ↓                 ↓
    AI Providers     Data Sources      Communication
    (Anthropic,      (GitHub,          (Slack, Twilio,
     OpenAI,          Linear,           Discord,
     Google AI)       Stripe)           Email)
         ↓                 ↓                 ↓
         └─────────────────┼─────────────────┘
                           ↓
    ┌──────────────────────┼──────────────────────┐
    ↓                      ↓                      ↓
 Vector DB            CASS/CM                   BV
 (memory)          (search/memory)       (task tracking)
```

### Key Integration Patterns

#### 1. Development Workflow
```
GitHub PR → Linear issue → Agent investigates →
  → Sentry errors + Datadog metrics →
  → Agent proposes fix → PR merged → Linear updated
```

#### 2. Customer Success Workflow
```
Zendesk ticket → Agent analyzes →
  → Searches knowledge base → Drafts response →
  → If complex: creates Linear issue →
  → Updates HubSpot contact record
```

#### 3. Revenue Operations Workflow
```
Stripe payment event → Agent correlates →
  → Updates HubSpot deal → Sends Twilio notification →
  → If failed: creates Zendesk ticket →
  → Generates report in Airtable
```

#### 4. Research Workflow
```
User question → Agent searches ArXiv + Semantic Scholar →
  → Downloads papers → AI summarizes →
  → Stores in Vector DB → Returns synthesis
```

---

## Implementation Roadmap

### Phase 1: Foundation (Weeks 1-4)
**Focus: Core agent capabilities**

- AI Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google AI
- Meta: Webhook Receiver, Cron
- Critical: Twitter, Linear, GitHub
- Storage: Vector DB, S3

### Phase 2: Productivity (Weeks 5-8)
**Focus: Business workflows**

- Enterprise: Microsoft 365, Jira
- Communication: Twilio, Slack
- CRM: HubSpot
- Observability: Sentry, Datadog, Grafana
- No-code: Airtable
- Design: Figma
- Payments: Stripe, Plaid

### Phase 3: Specialization (Weeks 9-12)
**Focus: Domain expertise**

- Infrastructure: Kubernetes, Terraform
- Research: ArXiv, Semantic Scholar, Anna's Archive
- Support: Zendesk
- Legal: DocuSign
- Security: 1Password

### Phase 4: Expansion (Weeks 13+)
**Focus: Long-tail value**

- Social: Reddit
- Media: Spotify, YouTube
- Home: Home Assistant
- Additional based on demand

---

## Quality Standards

### Every Connector Must:
1. **Pass FCP2 compliance runner** — Static and dynamic checks
2. **Have no real API calls in tests** — Mock-only unit tests
3. **Implement proper zones** — Capability-based security
4. **Support audit logging** — All operations tracked
5. **Handle rate limits** — Respect service quotas
6. **Document synergies** — Integration patterns explicit

### Bead Standards
Each connector bead must include:
- Strategic value score with justification
- Concrete agent use cases (not abstract)
- Flywheel synergies with specific connectors
- Zone architecture recommendations
- Capability model (read/write/admin)
- Representative operations with examples
- Testing approach
- Acceptance criteria

---

## Risk Considerations

### API Stability
- Services may change APIs
- Version pinning where possible
- Deprecation monitoring needed

### Rate Limits
- Different per service
- Budget enforcement at zone level
- Queue management for bursts

### Security
- Credentials never written to disk
- 1Password as credential source
- Audit trails mandatory

### Cost
- Some APIs charge per call
- AI providers charge per token
- Budget controls essential

---

## Success Metrics

### Coverage Metrics
- % of target workflows enabled
- # of connectors at each tier
- Cross-connector workflow coverage

### Quality Metrics
- FCP2 compliance rate
- Test coverage per connector
- API error rates

### Usage Metrics
- Operations per connector
- Workflow completion rates
- User satisfaction scores

---

## Success Criteria
- A stable scoring model exists (value, risk, effort, dependencies) and is applied consistently across the connector list.
- Tiering/prioritization outputs are actionable: they produce concrete connector epic work with dependencies that align to the FCP2 platform build order.
- The framework can be updated incrementally without losing rationale (future maintainers can understand why rankings changed).

 {"id":"flywheel_connectors-eqb","title":"fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates","description":"# fcp.homeassistant: Event subscription and real-time updates (FCP2)

## Goal
Provide streaming access to Home Assistant’s event bus via WebSocket:
- state_changed
- call_service
- custom events

## Scope
- Establish websocket session and authenticate.
- Subscribe to events with filters (domain/entity allowlists).
- Deliver events as FCP2 event envelopes with:
  - stable event IDs
  - correlation IDs

## Capability model (illustrative)
- `ha.events.subscribe` (sensitive)

## FCP2 requirements
- Single-zone binding.
- NetworkConstraints pinned (WS + HTTPS to HA host only).
- Streaming must be bounded/backpressured:
  - cap queue depth
  - drop/deny with explicit reason codes when overloaded
- Taint:
  - event payloads are tainted external input

## Deliverables
- Operation schema:
  - `ha.events.subscribe`
- Event envelope types:
  - `ha.event.state_changed`
  - `ha.event.call_service`

## Test strategy (MUST)
- Mock-only tests for:
  - reconnect + resubscribe
  - bounds/backpressure behavior
  - taint propagation

## Acceptance criteria
- Event streaming is reliable under reconnects and never causes unbounded memory growth.
